model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: Wisconsin has a sizable baseline quality edge in the efficiency ratings (KenPom #22 / Torvik #21) driven by an elite offense, while High Point profiles more like a fringe top-100 team with a defense that grades well below Wisconsin’s level. That gap matters in a one-game setting because Wisconsin can score efficiently even if the pace slows, and High Point’s most credible upset path requires hot three-point shooting plus forcing Wisconsin into an unusually sloppy turnover game. I didn’t find any clear, verified availability news that would meaningfully narrow the talent/efficiency gap, so I’m sticking with the higher-floor, higher-ceiling Badgers.

reasoningStep.title: West 5/12 — Wisconsin advances on efficiency and matchup floor
reasoningStep.summary: Wisconsin’s top-25 profile and elite offense make it the safer pick over a High Point team that can score but is much more vulnerable defensively against high-major competition.
evidence: Wisconsin ratings edge: KenPom #22 (AdjEM +23.4; AdjO 125.4 #11; AdjD 102.0) and Torvik #21 (AdjO 127.2 #8; AdjD rank 48).
High Point profile: KenPom #92 (AdjEM +8.4; AdjO 117.1 #66; AdjD 108.7) and Torvik #93 with a defense ranked 148th.
Upset path assessment: High Point needs high-variance outcomes (3PT shooting/turnovers) because its defensive efficiency suggests difficulty stringing together stops if Wisconsin plays to form.
Availability check: no specific, widely reported injury/availability red flag found in quick web/ESPN scan that would materially change the baseline gap.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.

pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
